heading: Direct acceptance of gifts by members of the armed forces and Department of Defense and Coast Guard employees and their families

children.label: A
children.text: A member of the armed forces described in subsection (b).

children.label: B
children.text: A civilian employee of the Department of Defense or Coast Guard described in subsection (c).

children.label: C
children.text: The family members of such a member or employee.

children.label: D
children.text: Survivors of such a member or employee who is killed.

children.label: A
children.text: apply uniformly to all elements of the Department of Defense and, to the maximum extent feasible, to the Coast Guard; and

children.label: B
children.text: require review and approval by a designated agency ethics official before acceptance of a gift to ensure that acceptance of the gift complies with the Joint Ethics Regulation.
as described in ;section 1413a(e)(2) of this title
in an operation or area designated as a combat operation or a combat zone, respectively, by the Secretary of Defense in accordance with the regulations prescribed under subsection (a);

that results in enrollment in a Warriors in Transition program, as defined in section 738(e) of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 2013 (; note); orPublic Law 112–23910 U.S.C. 1071
under other circumstances determined by the Secretary concerned to warrant treatment analogous to members covered by paragraph (1).

children.heading: .—Covered Employees
children.label: c .—Covered Employees
children.text: This section applies to a civilian employee of the Department of Defense or Coast Guard who, while an employee on or after , incurred an injury or illness under a circumstance described in subsection (b).September 11, 2001

children.heading: .—Gifts From Certain Sources Prohibited
children.label: d .—Gifts From Certain Sources Prohibited
children.text: The regulations prescribed under subsection (a) may not authorize the acceptance of a gift from a foreign government or international organization or their agents.

children.heading: .—Application of Certain Regulations
children.label: e .—Application of Certain Regulations
children.text: To the extent provided in the regulations issued under subsection (a) to implement subsection (b)(2), the regulations shall apply to the acceptance of gifts received after , for injuries or illnesses incurred on or after .December 31, 2011September 11, 2001
